steps.step: Reconstruct entity graph from vendor registrations and invoices
steps.why: to map ownership and transaction flows

steps.step: Cross-reference identities across addresses, bank accounts, and tax IDs
Run statistical sweeps for duplicate invoices and cash structuring
Investigate each anomaly to exonerate or confirm fraud

anomaly.title: Apex Supplies (V-031) shares a registered address with employee Vikram Kulkarni (E-007)
anomaly.detail: Apex Supplies and Vikram Kulkarni share the exact registered address. 14 invoices totalling €332,000, all approved by E-007 (Vikram Kulkarni), 0% PO coverage, no tax ID.

payload.label: Apex Supplies (V-031) shares a registered address with employee Vikram Kulkarni (E-007)

anomaly.title: Deepak Verma (E-015) is paid to the same bank account as Vikram Kulkarni (E-007)
anomaly.detail: Two employees draw salary to one identical bank account. Deepak Verma has the thinner footprint (recent join / no other records) — a likely ghost employee whose pay is diverted to Vikram Kulkarni. €216,000 in payroll.

payload.label: Deepak Verma (E-015) is paid to the same bank account as Vikram Kulkarni (E-007)

anomaly.title: €87,400 paid 2× — INV-2025-0554 NEFT Vega Print Works - IN
anomaly.detail: The same €87,400 payment appears 2 times with no reversing credit note anywhere in the books — both debits reference the same invoice. Debits: "INV-2025-0554 NEFT Vega Print Works - INV-2025-0554" (BANK-STMT-2025-11) · "INV-2025-0554 NEFT Vega Print Works - INV-2025-0554" (BANK-STMT-2025-11). A likely duplicate-payment loss.

anomaly.title: €54,200 paid 2× — INV-2025-0229 NEFT Aravalli Components -
anomaly.detail: A duplicate payment of €54,200 — but a matching credit note reverses it. Likely a caught accounting error, not a loss. Verify the reversal. Debits: "INV-2025-0229 NEFT Aravalli Components - INV-2025-0229" (BANK-STMT-2025-07) · "INV-2025-0229 NEFT Aravalli Components - INV-2025-0229" (BANK-STMT-2025-07).

payload.query: €87,400 paid 2× — INV-2025-0554 NEFT Vega Print Works - IN
Apex Supplies (V-031) shares a registered address with employee Vikram Kulkarni

payload.text: Hypothesis: The second payment of €87,400 on 2025-11-13 is a fraudulent duplicate of the 2025-11-10 payment for invoice INV-2025-0554, with no offsetting credit note issued to recover the funds. Before accusing, I need to rule out: The two payments represent a legitimate split payment arrangement (e.g., 50/50 deposit and balance) authorized under PO-69000, or the invoice was legitimately re-issued/re-entered for a distinct service period despite the identical invoice number.

payload.query: Deepak Verma (E-015) is paid to the same bank account as Vikram Kulkarni (E-007)

payload.text: Hypothesis: V-031 is a shell company controlled by E-007, evidenced by the shared address, self-approval of invoices, and lack of purchase orders or tax IDs. Before accusing, I need to rule out: The address is a legitimate shared business center or coworking space where both the employee and the vendor independently lease space, and the vendor is a genuine sole proprietor whose invoices were processed under an emergency or verbal authorization protocol not yet digitized in the PO system.

payload.query: Retrieve Purchase Order PO-69000 and payment terms to check for split payment authorizatio
Retrieve lease agreements or tenant directory for 245 Avenue des Champs-Élysées to verify

payload.text: Hypothesis: The duplicate payment of €54,200 to Aravalli Components was a control error that was successfully reversed via credit note CR-1042, resulting in no net financial loss. Before accusing, I need to rule out: The credit note CR-1042 explicitly states the duplicate payment was identified during reconciliation and reversed, which constitutes a control catch rather than a fraud loss.

Hypothesis: Deepak Verma (E-015) is a fictitious employee created by Vikram Kulkarni (E-007) to divert €72,000/month into their shared bank account. Before accusing, I need to rule out: Deepak Verma is a legitimate new hire whose company email has not yet been provisioned, and he legitimately shares a joint bank account with his manager Vikram Kulkarni (e.g., as a spouse or close relative).

payload.query: Retrieve ledger entries for July 2025 showing the posting of credit note CR-1042 and the n
Retrieve onboarding checklist, signed employment contract, and IT provisioning logs for E-

payload.text: The bank statement confirms two identical payments for the same invoice reference with no offsetting credit note or legitimate business reason found in the books.
Cleared — €54,200 paid 2× — INV-2025-0229 NEFT Aravalli Components -.

anomaly.detail: A duplicate payment of €54,200 — but a matching credit note reverses it. Likely a caught accounting error, not a loss. Verify the reversal. Debits: "INV-2025-0229 NEFT Aravalli Components - INV-2025-0229" (BANK-STMT-2025-07) · "INV-2025-0229 NEFT Aravalli Components - INV-2025-0229" (BANK-STMT-2025-07).

payload.why: A matching credit note reverses this payment three days later — a caught accounting error, not a loss.

payload.summary: ✓ UPHELD by the Nemotron panel — 3/3 examiners upheld — The ledger shows two identical €87,400.00 payments for the same invoice with no credit note, reversal, or split-payment authorization in the cited documents, and the invoice's single liability structu

finding.statement: The ledger shows two identical payments of €87,400.00 for invoice INV-2025-0554 to Vega Print Works on 2025-11-10 and 2025-11-13. No credit note, reversal, or split-payment authorization exists in the retrieved books to explain the second disbursement, confirming a duplicate payment loss.

evidence.claim: Two identical debits of €87,400.00 for INV-2025-0554 were recorded on 2025-11-10 and 2025-11-13.
The invoice INV-2025-0554 represents a single liability for an annual license with no indication of split payments.
No credit note or reversal was issued to offset the second payment, as confirmed by the absence of such records in the retrieved credit notes and ledger.
The second payment was not authorized as a split payment, as no such terms exist in the invoice or purchase order reference.

nemotron.reasoning: 3/3 examiners upheld — The ledger shows two identical €87,400.00 payments for the same invoice with no credit note, reversal, or split-payment authorization in the cited documents, and the invoice's single liability structu

payload.text: The identical bank account shared between a manager and a subordinate with different addresses, combined with the complete absence of onboarding records and email provisioning for the subordinate, confirms the employee is fictitious and the salary is being diverted.

votes.reasoning: The evidence consistently shows Deepak Verma lacks a digital footprint, shares a bank account with his manager despite different home addresses, and has no employment documentation, all directly suppo

No plausible innocent explanation exists for the identical bank account with a different home address, absence of onboarding documentation, and missing digital footprint, as these collectively rule ou

The identical bank account with the approving manager, lack of any digital or physical employment records, and distinct home addresses together rule out innocent explanations, making the ghost employe

payload.summary: ✓ UPHELD by the Nemotron panel — 3/3 examiners upheld — The evidence consistently shows Deepak Verma lacks a digital footprint, shares a bank account with his manager despite different home addresses, and has no employment documentation, all directly suppo

finding.statement: Deepak Verma (E-015) is a confirmed ghost employee. The HR records show he shares an identical salary bank account with his manager, Vikram Kulkarni (E-007), despite having different home addresses, which rules out a legitimate shared household account. Furthermore, Deepak lacks a company email address while his peers have one, and no onboarding documents, contracts, or IT provisioning logs exist in the retrieved books to verify his physical existence or employment legitimacy. The combination of a missing digital footprint, a shared bank account with the approving manager, and the absence of any corroborating employment documentation confirms a payroll fraud scheme where Vikram Kulkarni diverts Deepak's salary.

evidence.claim: Deepak Verma (E-015) and Vikram Kulkarni (E-007) share the same salary bank account (BNP Paribas ****7731).
Deepak Verma has no company email on file, unlike other employees such as Vikram Kulkarni and Kavya Menon.
Deepak Verma's home address (40 Rue de Sèvres) is different from Vikram Kulkarni's (245 Avenue des Champs-Élysées), ruling out a shared household account.
No onboarding checklist, signed employment contract, or IT provisioning logs exist for Deepak Verma in the retrieved books, indicating a lack of physical existence or legitimate employment process.
Vikram Kulkarni is Deepak Verma's direct reporting manager, providing the opportunity to create and approve the ghost employee's payroll.

nemotron.reasoning: 3/3 examiners upheld — The evidence consistently shows Deepak Verma lacks a digital footprint, shares a bank account with his manager despite different home addresses, and has no employment documentation, all directly suppo

payload.text: The combination of shared address, self-approval, missing POs, and missing tax ID, coupled with the absence of any document supporting the innocent explanation (shared business center), confirms the shell company scheme.

votes.reasoning: The evidence shows identical addresses, self-approval of all invoices, absence of purchase orders, lack of tax ID, and no innocent explanation found, matching the textbook shell company pattern.

The evidence of identical registered address to employee's home, self-approval of all invoices, absence of purchase orders, lack of tax ID, and no documentation supporting a shared business center con

The identical address, self-approval of all invoices, absence of purchase orders, missing tax ID, and lack of supporting lease documentation constitute a textbook shell company scheme with no innocent

payload.summary: ✓ UPHELD by the Nemotron panel — 3/3 examiners upheld — The evidence shows identical addresses, self-approval of all invoices, absence of purchase orders, lack of tax ID, and no innocent explanation found, matching the textbook shell company pattern.

finding.statement: Apex Supplies (V-031) is a confirmed shell company controlled by employee Vikram Kulkarni (E-007), evidenced by the shared address, self-approval of all invoices, absence of purchase orders, and lack of tax ID, with no innocent explanation found in the retrieved documents.

evidence.claim: Vendor V-031 and Employee E-007 share the exact registered address 245 Avenue des Champs-Élysées, Paris 75008
All invoices were approved by E-007 (Vikram Kulkarni), indicating self-approval
No purchase orders exist for any of the invoices
Vendor lacks a valid tax ID/VAT number
No lease agreements or tenant directories were found to support the innocent explanation of a shared business center

nemotron.reasoning: 3/3 examiners upheld — The evidence shows identical addresses, self-approval of all invoices, absence of purchase orders, lack of tax ID, and no innocent explanation found, matching the textbook shell company pattern.
